senses.definition: Having come to rest or been brought to a resting position (rare and nonstandard).
examples: The machinery lay restung after the long night of work.
By dawn the sea seemed restung, its waves finally calm.
His mind, once restless, felt strangely restung and quiet.

senses.definition: Toward rest or toward a place of rest.
examples: The tired hikers moved restward as the sun went down.
After hours of work, my thoughts drifted restward.
The ship turned restward, back toward the harbor and safety.

senses.definition: Going toward rest or a resting place.
examples: Their restward journey finally ended at a quiet inn.
The soldiers welcomed the restward march after weeks at the front.
We followed a restward path that led us out of the noise of the city.

senses.definition: Toward rest or toward a place of rest (very rare, mainly a variant of “restward”).
examples: The exhausted army marched restwards after the battle.
As night fell, the village seemed to turn restwards and close its doors.
We sailed restwards, seeking a calm bay to anchor in.

senses.definition: Stubborn or unwilling to move; restless and resisting control (archaic or dialect).
examples: The resty horse refused to go any farther down the path.
He grew resty in his chair and could not sit still through the long speech.
The children became resty after hours of waiting.

senses.definition: To change the style, design, or appearance of something so it looks different or more attractive.
examples: She decided to restyle her hair before the job interview.
The company plans to restyle its logo to appeal to younger customers.
They restyled the old apartment with modern furniture and colors.

senses.definition: To give someone or something a new name or title.
examples: The magazine restyled the column as "Living Well" to attract more readers.
After moving to the city, he restyled himself with a shorter, more modern name.
The brand restyled the product line under a single, simpler name.

senses.definition: Having had its style or appearance changed.
examples: The restyled car has a more sporty front end.
She showed off her restyled kitchen with new cabinets and lights.
Customers loved the restyled packaging and clearer label.

senses.definition: Third person singular form of “restyle”: changes the style, design, or appearance of something.
examples: The salon restyles hundreds of clients’ hair every month.
Each year the designer restyles the collection to match new trends.
The app restyles the layout automatically when you rotate the screen.

senses.definition: Present participle of “restyle”: changing the style, design, or appearance of something.
examples: They are restyling the website to make it easier to use.
She spent the weekend restyling old clothes instead of buying new ones.
The company is restyling its stores with brighter colors and open spaces.

senses.definition: The act of changing the style, design, or appearance of something.
examples: A complete restyling of the brand helped increase sales.
The restyling of the living room made the space feel much larger.
Hair restyling is one of the most popular services at the salon.

senses.definition: To put someone or something under control, power, or influence again (very rare, mainly formal or historical).
examples: The new rulers tried to resubject the region after the brief revolt.
They feared any peace deal would resubject them to foreign control.
The policy could resubject small farmers to heavy taxes.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of “resubject”: put under control or influence again.
examples: After the brief uprising, the province was resubjected by force.
They refused to be resubjected to the old discriminatory laws.
The community was resubjected to strict military rule.

senses.definition: Present participle of “resubject”: putting someone or something under control again.
examples: The government is resubjecting the area to direct rule.
Resubjecting workers to the old contract would remove many protections.
They warned against resubjecting the region to military control.

senses.definition: The act of bringing someone or something back under control, power, or influence.
examples: The treaty led to the resubjection of the small kingdom.
Activists opposed the resubjection of citizens to emergency powers.
The novel tells the story of a people’s resubjection after a failed revolution.

senses.definition: Third person singular of “resubject”: puts someone or something back under control or influence.
examples: The new law effectively resubjects citizens to strict censorship.
This policy resubjects many villages to central authority.
The agreement resubjects workers to the old pay scale.

senses.definition: To bring people or a place under control or domination again, especially by force.
examples: The empire tried to resubjugate the territory after the rebellion.
They feared any compromise would resubjugate them to colonial rule.
The army moved in to resubjugate the breakaway region.

senses.definition: To cause a substance to go through sublimation again (highly technical and rare).
examples: The chemist decided to resublimate the crystals to increase purity.
If necessary, resublimate the sample to remove any remaining impurities.
They resublimated the compound under lower pressure.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of “resublimate”: underwent or caused sublimation again.
examples: The resublimated product showed far fewer contaminants.
Once resublimated, the substance formed pure white crystals.
They analyzed the resublimated material for trace metals.

senses.definition: Present participle of “resublimate”: undergoing or causing sublimation again.
examples: Resublimating the compound at a lower temperature improved the result.
They spent the afternoon resublimating several batches of the chemical.
Resublimating and filtering the mixture produced a much purer sample.

senses.definition: The process of sublimating a substance again to improve its purity (technical).
examples: Resublimation is often used to purify sensitive organic compounds.
Careful resublimation can remove colored impurities from the solid.
The protocol includes an optional resublimation step for higher purity.

senses.definition: To sublime again, or to cause something to go directly from solid to gas again (very rare, technical).
examples: If impurities remain, resublime the solid under reduced pressure.
The material may decompose if you try to resublime it too quickly.
They chose to resublime the mixture rather than recrystallize it.

senses.definition: To put or go under the surface of water or liquid again.
examples: After checking the cable, the diver will resubmerge the equipment.
They had to resubmerge the bottles to keep them cool.
The submarine plans to resubmerge once repairs are complete.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of “resubmerge”: went under water again or put something under water again.
examples: The diver briefly surfaced, then resubmerged to continue the inspection.
They resubmerged the samples in cold water.
The structure slowly resubmerged as the tide came in.

senses.definition: Present participle of “resubmerge”: going or putting something under water again.
examples: He is resubmerging the equipment to test it at a deeper level.
Resubmerging the jars kept the temperature constant.
The whale appeared briefly before resubmerging.

senses.definition: The act of submitting something again, especially after it has been changed.
examples: The journal allows one resubmission after major revisions.
Please attach a note explaining any changes made since the last resubmission.
Resubmission of the form is required if any details were incorrect.

senses.definition: Plural of “resubmission”: several cases of submitting something again.
examples: The editor received many resubmissions after the reviewers’ comments.
Multiple resubmissions may delay the approval process.
We charge a small fee for resubmissions made after the deadline.

senses.definition: To submit something again, often after you have corrected or changed it.
examples: You must resubmit your application with the missing documents.
The authors plan to revise the article and resubmit it to another journal.
Please resubmit the form if any of the information has changed.

senses.definition: Third person singular of “resubmit”: submits something again.
examples: If the system rejects the file, it automatically resubmits it.
She corrects her mistakes and resubmits the assignment.
The company resubmits its bid every year.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of “resubmit”: submitted again.
examples: She resubmitted her visa application with all the required documents.
The corrected manuscript was resubmitted to the journal.
They resubmitted the claim after adding more information.

senses.definition: Present participle of “resubmit”: submitting something again.
examples: We are resubmitting the proposal with a lower budget.
Resubmitting the form online is faster than sending it by mail.
She is resubmitting her application after adding more details.

senses.definition: To place someone or something under the authority of another person or group again (rare, formal).
examples: The reform does not resubordinate local councils to the central ministry.
They refused to resubordinate the agency to political control.
The proposal would resubordinate the unit under regional command.

senses.definition: To subscribe again, for example to a service, magazine, or online plan.
examples: You can resubscribe to the newsletter at any time.
Many users resubscribe after trying the premium features.
She chose not to resubscribe when the price increased.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of “resubscribe”: subscribed again.
examples: After a short break, he resubscribed to the streaming service.
She resubscribed when she saw the new discount.
They resubscribed to the journal for another year.

senses.definition: A person who subscribes again to a service, product, or publication.
examples: Former customers are offered a discount as resubscribers.
Each resubscriber receives a special welcome-back email.
Resubscribers often spend more than first-time users.

senses.definition: Third person singular of “resubscribe”: subscribes again.
examples: If a user resubscribes within 30 days, their data is restored.
She often cancels and then resubscribes when there is a promotion.
The customer resubscribes every year without fail.

senses.definition: Present participle of “resubscribe”: subscribing again.
examples: Resubscribing is easy; just click the link in the email.
Many customers are resubscribing after the latest update.
She is thinking about resubscribing to the magazine.

senses.definition: The act of subscribing again to a service, product, or publication.
examples: Resubscription is available at a reduced price for former members.
They launched a resubscription campaign to win back old customers.
We send reminders before the resubscription date.

senses.definition: To provide new or additional evidence to support something again (rare, mainly legal or academic).
examples: The researcher tried to resubstantiate the original claim with new data.
They failed to resubstantiate the allegations in court.
Further experiments are needed to resubstantiate these findings.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of “resubstantiate”: supported again with new or further evidence.
examples: The theory was resubstantiated by recent studies.
They successfully resubstantiated their claims during the appeal.
Only a few parts of the report could be resubstantiated.

senses.definition: Present participle of “resubstantiate”: supporting a claim again with new or further evidence.
examples: The team is resubstantiating the original hypothesis with updated data.
Resubstantiating these accusations will not be easy.
They focused on resubstantiating the most important results.

senses.definition: The act of supporting a claim again with new or further evidence (rare, formal).
examples: The paper offered a resubstantiation of the earlier results.
No clear resubstantiation of the charges was presented.
Their argument depends on the resubstantiation of several key assumptions.

senses.definition: To substitute something again, or to replace one thing with another for a second time (rare, technical).
examples: If the calculation is wrong, resubstitute the correct values into the equation.
They chose to resubstitute the original material after testing the new one.
You may need to resubstitute the variable to simplify the expression.

senses.definition: The act of substituting again, especially in mathematics or technical contexts.
examples: Resubstitution of the values gives the final result.
The method uses resubstitution to check the solution.
Frequent resubstitution of materials slowed down the experiment.

senses.definition: To succeed again, especially after already having success before (rare).
examples: After winning his first title, he hoped to resucceed the following year.
The company managed to resucceed in a very competitive market.
It is difficult to resucceed once expectations are so high.

senses.definition: To suck again, or to draw something in again by sucking (rare, often humorous or informal).
examples: The baby would cry, stop, and then resuck the bottle.
He had to resuck the drink through the straw after spilling it.
The device can resuck the liquid back into the container.

senses.definition: The act or process of sweating again, or giving out fluid again through the skin (very rare, technical).
examples: The patient showed resudation after the medication wore off.
Resudation of moisture on the surface indicated a leak.
They monitored any resudation during the treatment.

senses.definition: Likely a misspelling or very rare form of “reuse”: to use something again.
examples: They chose to resue the old bottles instead of buying new ones.
You can resue these containers for storing leftovers.
He tried to resue the code in another project.

senses.definition: To suffer again, or to experience pain, loss, or difficulty once more.
examples: He feared he would resuffer the same injuries if he returned to the sport.
Victims often resuffer trauma when they tell their stories.
She did not want to resuffer that kind of disappointment.

senses.definition: The state or act of suffering again; repeated suffering (very rare, literary or archaic).
examples: His life seemed a long resufferance of earlier wounds.
The poem describes the resufferance of lost love.
She dreaded the resufferance of old humiliations.

senses.definition: To suggest something again, especially if it was not accepted before.
examples: She decided to resuggest her idea at the next meeting.
If they reject your proposal, you can always resuggest it later.
He did not want to resuggest the plan after it was ignored.

senses.definition: The act of suggesting something again.
examples: Her resuggestion of the idea finally received attention.
The manager welcomed the resuggestion of cost-cutting measures.
After some discussion, a resuggestion of the earlier plan was made.

senses.definition: Likely a misspelling or nonstandard form of “re-suing” or “reusing”; most often intended as “reusing”: using something again.
examples: Resuing old jars is better for the environment.
He focused on resuing materials instead of throwing them away.
The project encourages resuing and recycling household items.
